GenoSim 1.0.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Introduction to GenoSim: Forward-Time Genotype Simulation

GenoSim: Forward-Time Genotype Simulator for Clinical and Population Genetics

Source: R/GenoSim-package.R
GenoSim-package.Rd

GenoSim provides a complete pipeline for simulating diploid SNP genotype data in settings where real human NGS data are scarce due to ethical, budgetary, or regulatory constraints. It implements biologically realistic forward-time population genetics models and supports two simulation modes:

Population mode (simulate_population): Generates founder genotypes from allele frequencies and propagates them forward for up to 10 generations using Wright-Fisher drift, directional selection, mutation, and recombination.

Pedigree mode (simulate_from_pedigree): Reads real family VCF files and a pedigree CSV, reconstructs phased haplotypes from observed genotypes, propagates them through the exact family structure respecting inbreeding coefficients and recombination, and optionally appends additional synthetic generations.

Main functions

simulate_population

Forward population simulator (no pedigree)

simulate_from_pedigree

Pedigree-constrained VCF-based simulator

read_vcf_cohort

Read family VCFs into a dosage matrix

read_pedigree

Read and validate a pedigree CSV

hwe_test

Per-SNP Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium test

compute_ld

Pairwise linkage disequilibrium (r?? and D')

detect_roh

Runs of homozygosity detection

run_pca

Principal component analysis on genotype matrix

compute_fst

Weir-Cockerham FST between generations

diversity_metrics

Nei diversity, Watterson's theta

export_vcf

Export simulated genotypes to VCF

export_plink

Export to PLINK PED/MAP/RAW

export_csv

Export to tidy CSV files

plot_dashboard

Six-panel summary dashboard

plot_pedigree_tree

Draw family pedigree

plot_af_trajectory

Allele frequency trajectories

plot_kinship_heatmap

Kinship coefficient heatmap

Example data

The package ships example data accessible via example_pedigree and example_vcf_dir, representing a 25-member, 4-generation consanguineous family (FAM_KHAN) with 200 SNPs across chromosomes 1-5.

References

  • Wright S (1931) Evolution in Mendelian populations. Genetics 16:97???159.

  • Weir BS, Cockerham CC (1984) Estimating F-statistics for the analysis of population structure. Evolution 38:1358???1370.

  • Nei M (1973) Analysis of gene diversity in subdivided populations. PNAS 70:3321???3323.

See also
